name: Stars of the South: Parrissal de Beceite Trail
name_original: El Parrizal de Beceite - Ida y vuelta por els Parque Natural dels Ports
description: Hike the El Parrizal de Beceite trail, a difficult 11.9-mile (19.2 km) route through the Matarraña River gorge with wooden walkways.
region_intro: Hiking around Beceite offers diverse natural landscapes, characterized by rugged mountains, verdant forests, and crystal-clear rivers. The region features impressive river gorges with spectacular limestone formations, such as those found along the Matarraña River. Hikers can explore mountainous areas like the Els Ports massif, which provides challenging terrain and panoramic views. This varied topography makes Beceite a prime destination for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around Beceite The most popular hiking route is Route of Parrissal through the walkways of the Natural Path of Matarraña Algars, a 4.5 miles (7.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 30 minutes to complete. This moderate route features wooden walkways spanning clear pools and winding paths through rocky terrain. Another top favourite among local hikers is Peñagalera Ascent Trail (PR-TE 151), a difficult 11.8 miles (19.0 km) path. This challenging circular hike provides extensive views over the Els Ports massif and the Ulldemó River valley. Local hikers also love the Matarraña-Algars Nature Trail from Beceite, a 8.2 miles (13.2 km) trail leading through river valleys and natural landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 45 minutes. Hiking around Beceite is defined by river gorges, limestone formations, and mountainous terrain. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to challenging multi-day treks. The routes in Beceite are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 220 reviews. More than 2000 hikers have used komoot to explore Beceite's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Beceite?
A: Beceite offers a wide variety of hiking experiences with over 60 trails to explore. These routes cater to different fitness levels, from easy walks to challenging multi-day treks through diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there any easy hiking routes suitable for families or beginners?
A: Yes, Beceite has 14 easy hiking routes perfect for families and beginners. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974413961/route-of-parrissal-through-the-walkways-of-the-natural-path-of-matarrana-algars" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of Parrissal through the walkways of the Natural Path of Matarraña Algars&lt;/a&gt; is a popular choice, known for its wooden walkways over clear pools and accessible paths through rocky terrain, making it suitable for all ages.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see on hikes in Beceite?
A: Beceite is rich in natural beauty, featuring impressive river gorges like the Matarraña River gorge with its spectacular limestone formations known as 'Gubies'. You'll also find numerous natural pools, three prominent rivers (Matarraña, Ulldemó, and Pena), and mountainous areas such as the Els Ports massif. The region is characterized by endless forests, unique rock formations, and occasional caves and waterfalls.

Q: Are there any trails that feature waterfalls or natural pools for swimming?
A: Yes, Beceite is known for its beautiful waterways. The Natural Area of La Pesquera, formed by the Ulldemó River, offers picturesque landscapes with natural pools. You can also find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2479086" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt de Robert Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, a notable highlight in the area, which can be reached via various trails.

Q: What are some challenging hikes for experienced trekkers in Beceite?
A: For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e981149235/penagalera-ascent-trail-pr-te-151" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñagalera Ascent Trail (PR-TE 151)&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 11.8-mile (19.0 km) circular hike offering extensive views over the Els Ports massif and the Ulldemó River valley. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e953803638/stars-of-the-south-parrissal-de-beceite-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Stars of the South: Parrissal de Beceite Trail&lt;/a&gt;, also rated difficult.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Beceite?
A: Yes, many trails in Beceite are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e981149235/penagalera-ascent-trail-pr-te-151" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñagalera Ascent Trail (PR-TE 151)&lt;/a&gt; is a notable circular option. Additionally, the region offers routes like the Pozas del Río Matarraña Circular Route, which explores natural pools and historical sites.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Beceite?
A: Beceite is enjoyable for hiking throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for exploring. During warmer months, the natural pools and rivers provide refreshing swimming opportunities, making them particularly appealing.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Beceite?
A: The hiking routes in Beceite are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 220 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse terrain, stunning natural landscapes, and the well-maintained paths, especially those along the river gorges.

Q: Are there any specific landmarks or viewpoints to look out for?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/688369" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penyagalera Summit (1,035 m)&lt;/a&gt; offers spectacular panoramic views over the Els Ports massif and the Ulldemó River valley. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4978626" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parrissal Walkways&lt;/a&gt; themselves are a unique landmark, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4961316" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Els Estrets Gorge&lt;/a&gt; is another impressive natural formation.

Q: Is there parking available for popular trailheads, especially for El Parrizal?
A: Yes, for popular areas like El Parrizal de Beceite, parking is typically available. It's advisable to arrive early during peak seasons to secure a spot, as this area can attract many visitors due to its scenic beauty and accessible routes.

Q: Can I combine hiking with other outdoor activities in Beceite?
A: Beceite is a hub for various outdoor pursuits beyond hiking. You can also enjoy mountain biking, horse riding, cycling the Via Verde of Val del Zafán, and kayaking in the Pantano de Pena. The region's diverse landscape supports a wide range of activities.

Q: Are there any multi-day hiking options in the Beceite region?
A: For serious hikers, El Gran Paseo de los Puertos de Beceite is an 86 km multi-day route, typically completed in 4 days. This immersive trek traverses some of the wildest mountain massifs of the Iberian Peninsula, linking villages across Aragon, Valencia, and Catalonia.

The Parrissal gouges get their name because the silhouettes of the rock set resemble these sharp tools that allow carpenters and cabinetmakers to work with wood.
From the top of the Romeret pass they can be seen very well.

Formed by three semicircular arches, separated by pillars. It is built with ashlar at the base and arches and with limestone and rough stone masonry for the rest of the work. It is a construction of about 30.00 m in length, 1.20 in width and 6.00 m in height that crosses the ravine of the Preachers.
The road to Parrissal just passes by it.

faqs.question: How long does it take to hike El Parrizal de Beceite - Round trip through els Parque Natural dels Ports?
faqs.answer: This round trip hike typically takes around 5 hours and 45 minutes to complete. However, your actual duration may vary depending on your pace and how much time you spend enjoying the scenery and highlights.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this trail?
faqs.answer: The full round trip is classified as difficult. While initial sections are relatively easy, other parts involve abrupt terrain, narrow footbridges, and river crossings on stepping stones. Good fitness and appropriate footwear are essential.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers diverse terrain, primarily following the Matarraña River. You'll encounter wooden walkways suspended over the river, tunnels carved into rock, lush forests, and impressive vertical rock walls known as 'Gubies'. Expect sections with stepping stone river crossings and an initial 800-meter unpaved botanical route.

faqs.question: What are the notable landmarks or features along the way?
faqs.answer: The route is rich in natural and historical features. You'll pass by the beautiful Pozas del Mas de Lluvia, navigate through the stunning Gúbies del Parrissal, and reach the dramatic El Parrizal Gorge End Point, also known as 'Los Estrechos'. You can also see the Aqueduct of the Sèquia Major, ancient 'Fenellosa' cave paintings, and old coal mine structures at 'Pla de la Mina'.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is popular year-round, but spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be very hot, and some sections might be more challenging after heavy rains due to increased water levels.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for El Parrizal de Beceite?
faqs.answer: Yes, due to its popularity and protected status within the Matarraña River Nature Reserve, access to El Parrizal is regulated. You often need to book tickets online in advance, especially for parking, and adhere to specific entry times to manage capacity. It's advisable to check the official local tourism website before your visit.

faqs.question: Is the trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: No, pets are not allowed on this route. This regulation is in place to protect the delicate ecosystem of the Matarraña River Nature Reserve.

faqs.question: Where is the trailhead and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trailhead is located near Beceite. Parking is available, but it is regulated and often requires advance booking, especially during peak seasons. It's crucial to secure your parking spot online beforehand to ensure access.

faqs.question: What regulations should I be aware of when visiting Parc Natural dels Ports?
faqs.answer: As this trail is within the Parc Natural dels Ports, it's important to respect its regulations. Bathing in the Matarraña River is strictly prohibited as the water is used for human consumption. Pets are not allowed on the trail, and visitors must adhere to any access regulations, including pre-booked tickets and parking, to help preserve the natural environment.

faqs.question: What should I wear and bring for the hike?
faqs.answer: Given the varied terrain and potential for wet crossings, appropriate footwear with good grip is essential. It's highly recommended to bring spare shoes or sandals for river crossings, as slips are common. Dress in layers suitable for hiking, carry enough water, snacks, and sun protection. Remember, bathing in the river is not permitted.

faqs.question: Is the initial section of the trail suitable for strollers or wheelchairs?
faqs.answer: Yes, the initial 800-meter unpaved section of the route is suitable for strollers and wheelchairs. This part also serves as a botanical route and provides access to the 'Fenellosa' cave paintings.
